meta.title: The Great Commission and Ascension
meta.verseText: Always be joyful because you belong to the Lord. I will say it again. Be joyful!
meta.theme: Sharing the good news about Jesus with the whole world

questions.text: Where did Jesus tell His disciples to meet Him after He rose from the dead?
questions.hint: It was a high place in a region called Galilee.
questions.explanation: Jesus told His disciples to meet Him on a mountain in Galilee, where He gave them the Great Commission.

questions.text: What book of the Bible tells us about the Great Commission?
questions.hint: It's the first book in the New Testament.
questions.explanation: The Great Commission is found in Matthew 28:16-20.

questions.text: True or False: Jesus said all authority in heaven and on earth had been given to Him.
questions.hint: Jesus is King over everything.
questions.explanation: Jesus said, 'All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me' (Matthew 28:18).

questions.text: How many days did Jesus spend with His friends after Easter?
questions.hint: It's the same number of days and nights it rained on Noah's ark.
questions.explanation: During the forty days after Easter, Jesus spent time with His friends, teaching them and helping them understand God's plan.

questions.text: What did the disciples do when they saw Jesus alive on the mountain?
questions.hint: They knew He was the Son of God.
questions.explanation: When the disciples saw Jesus, they worshiped Him because they knew He was truly the Son of God.

questions.text: Jesus told His disciples to make _____ of all nations.
questions.hint: A disciple is a follower who learns from a teacher.
questions.explanation: Jesus said, 'Go and make disciples of all nations,' meaning to help people everywhere follow Him.

questions.text: True or False: Some of the disciples still had doubts even when they saw Jesus.
questions.hint: The Bible is honest about their struggles.
questions.explanation: Matthew 28:17 tells us that when they saw Him, they worshiped Him, but some still had their doubts.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Commission'?
options: Jesus' command to go and make disciples of all nations
questions.hint: It's the big mission Jesus gave His followers.
questions.explanation: The Great Commission is Jesus' command to go and make disciples of all nations, teaching them about Him.

questions.text: In whose name did Jesus say to baptize new disciples?
options: The Father, Son, and Holy Spirit
questions.hint: Three persons of the Trinity.
questions.explanation: Jesus said to baptize them 'in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit' (Matthew 28:19).

questions.text: What promise did Jesus give His disciples at the end of the Great Commission?
options: He would always be with them
questions.hint: It's a promise of presence.
questions.explanation: Jesus promised, 'I am always with you' (Matthew 28:20), meaning we are never alone.

questions.text: What is our Bottom Line this week?
options: You can share the good news about Jesus
questions.hint: It's about sharing news about Jesus.
questions.explanation: Our Bottom Line is: You can share the good news about Jesus!

questions.text: What is the 'ascension'?
options: When Jesus left Earth and went to heaven
questions.hint: It happened after the Great Commission.
questions.explanation: The ascension is when Jesus left Earth and went up to be with God in heaven.

questions.text: Where is our monthly memory verse found?
questions.hint: It's in a letter Paul wrote.
questions.explanation: Our memory verse is Philippians 4:4: 'Always be joyful because you belong to the Lord. I will say it again. Be joyful!'

questions.text: Fill in the blank: 'Always be _____ because you belong to the Lord.'
questions.hint: It's a happy word that goes deeper than happiness.
questions.explanation: Philippians 4:4 says, 'Always be joyful because you belong to the Lord.'

questions.text: Who helps us when we share the good news about Jesus?
options: No one, we do it alone
questions.hint: God sent a Helper to live in us.
questions.explanation: The Holy Spirit lives in us, gives us courage, and helps us know the right words to say.

questions.text: True or False: Jesus wants us to share the good news only in our own country.
questions.hint: Jesus said 'all nations.'
questions.explanation: Jesus told His disciples to make disciples of ALL nations — the good news is for the whole world!

questions.text: What are two ways we can share about Jesus?
options: With our words and the way we live
questions.hint: Showing AND telling.
questions.explanation: We share about Jesus through the words we speak AND the way we live — showing His love in kindness, forgiveness, and honesty.

questions.text: What does it mean that Jesus has 'all authority'?
options: He is King over everything in heaven and earth
questions.hint: Authority means power and rule.
questions.explanation: Having all authority means Jesus is King over everything — there is no one greater than Him in heaven or on earth.

questions.text: Why can we have joy even when things are hard?
options: Because nothing bad happens to us
Because Jesus is with us and never changes
Because we get everything we want
questions.hint: Joy is different from just being happy.
questions.explanation: Real joy comes from knowing Jesus is with us always. It doesn't depend on circumstances because Jesus never changes.

questions.text: How can you share Jesus at your school?
options: Yell at kids who don't know Jesus
Include others, be honest, and be a good friend
Only talk to your best friend
questions.hint: Think about showing Jesus' love.
questions.explanation: You can share Jesus at school by including others, being honest, and helping someone who needs a friend — showing His love.

questions.text: What should you do when you feel nervous about telling someone about Jesus?
options: Ask the Holy Spirit for courage
questions.hint: Remember who helps you.
questions.explanation: The Holy Spirit gives us courage when we feel nervous and helps us know the right words to say.

questions.text: Which of these shows Jesus' love to others?
options: Being mean to people who are different
questions.hint: Act like Jesus.
questions.explanation: When we are kind, forgiving, and loving like Jesus, people see Him through us.

questions.text: Your neighbor is new and has no friends. What would Jesus want you to do?
options: Welcome them and be their friend
questions.hint: Be a friend like Jesus.
questions.explanation: Jesus wants us to include and love others — being a friend is one great way to show His love.

questions.text: Fill in the blank: 'I am always _____ you,' said Jesus.
questions.hint: A promise of never being alone.
questions.explanation: Jesus promised, 'I am always with you' — we are never alone when we follow Him.

questions.text: True or False: We have to share about Jesus all by ourselves.
questions.hint: Jesus promised to send a Helper.
questions.explanation: We are never alone! The Holy Spirit is always with us to help, give us courage, and guide our words.

questions.text: What did Jesus mean when He said to teach people to 'obey everything I have commanded'?
options: Follow Jesus' teachings with their whole lives
questions.hint: It's about how we live every day.
questions.explanation: Jesus wants His disciples to follow His teachings with their whole hearts and whole lives — not just say the right things.

questions.text: Fill in the blank: The good news about Jesus is for _____.
questions.hint: Jesus said 'all nations.'
questions.explanation: Jesus told His followers to make disciples of ALL nations — the good news is meant to be spread all around the world.

questions.text: What is one way you can share the good news with your family this week?
options: Pray together or tell them something you learned about Jesus
questions.hint: Home is a great place to start.
questions.explanation: Your home is a place where you can show kindness, forgiveness, and love like Jesus — and tell your family about Him.

questions.text: Who gives us power and wisdom to share about Jesus?
questions.hint: The third person of the Trinity.
questions.explanation: The Holy Spirit gives us power, courage, and wisdom to love others and tell them about Jesus.

questions.text: True or False: You're too young to share about Jesus with others.
questions.hint: Everyone who follows Jesus has this mission.
questions.explanation: No one is too young! Jesus gives this mission to everyone who follows Him — kids can share about Jesus too!

questions.text: Which of these is NOT part of the Great Commission?
questions.hint: Jesus wanted the news to SPREAD.
questions.explanation: Jesus told us to go, baptize, and teach — never to keep the good news a secret!

questions.text: How does showing kindness help others see Jesus?
options: It reflects Jesus' love and character
It only helps if they ask
questions.hint: When you act like Jesus, people see Him.
questions.explanation: When you're kind, honest, and forgiving like Jesus, the world gets to see what Jesus is like through you.

termPairs.definition: Jesus' command to go and make disciples of all nations
When Jesus left Earth and went up to heaven
A follower of Jesus who learns from Him
The region where the disciples met Jesus on a mountain
God's Helper who lives in believers and gives courage
Power and rule — Jesus has it over heaven and earth
To dip in water as a sign of following Jesus
The verse where Jesus says 'go and make disciples of all nations'
Our memory verse about always being joyful
A deep gladness from knowing Jesus that stays even when things are hard
The time Jesus spent with His disciples after Easter
The Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit — one God in three persons
The message that Jesus died and rose to rescue us

keyWords.hint: The 'Great ___' — Jesus' big mission for His followers
When Jesus went up to heaven
Followers of Jesus who learn from Him
The region where Jesus met His disciples on a mountain
The high place where the disciples met Jesus
Jesus said to make disciples of all ___
Power and rule — Jesus has all of it
To dip in water as a sign of following Jesus
How we should always be because we belong to the Lord

The Holy ___ gives us courage and wisdom
What the disciples did when they saw Jesus alive
The book of the Bible where the Great Commission is found
The Son of God who gave us the Great Commission
What the Holy Spirit gives us when we feel nervous

story.summary: After Jesus rose from the dead, He spent forty days with His disciples. One day, He told them to meet Him on a mountain in Galilee. When they saw Him alive, they worshiped Him — though some still had doubts. Jesus declared that all authority in heaven and earth had been given to Him, and He gave them the Great Commission: go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit, and teaching them to obey everything Jesus commanded. He promised, 'I am always with you.' Then Jesus ascended into heaven to be with God the Father. Today, followers of Jesus continue the mission — sharing the good news with their words and their lives, empowered by the Holy Spirit.

scenes.title: Meeting on the Mountain
scenes.description: After Easter, Jesus spent forty days with His disciples. He told them to meet Him on a mountain in Galilee. When they saw Him alive, they worshiped Him — but some still had doubts.

scenes.title: All Authority Given
scenes.description: Jesus came to His disciples and declared, 'All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.' He was showing them that He is King over everything.

scenes.title: The Great Commission
scenes.description: Jesus gave His followers a mission: 'Go and make disciples of all nations. Baptize them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit. Teach them to obey everything I have commanded you.'

scenes.title: The Promise and the Ascension
scenes.description: Jesus promised His followers, 'I am always with you.' Then He ascended to heaven to be with God. The Holy Spirit would come to give them power to share the good news.

scenes.title: Our Mission Today
scenes.description: The good news about Jesus is for the whole world. We share it with our words and the way we live — showing kindness, forgiveness, and love wherever we go.
